question_text: A balloon is filled with helium gas at room temperature. The gas particles inside the balloon are moving rapidly and colliding with the balloon walls. Which statement best explains why the balloon stays inflated?

options.label: A
options.text: The helium particles stick to the rubber and hold it in place

options.label: B
options.text: The continuous collisions of gas particles with the balloon walls create an outward force called pressure

options.label: C
options.text: The rubber material of the balloon naturally expands when it comes into contact with gas

options.label: D
options.text: Helium particles are heavier than air so they push outward on the balloon walls

correct_answer: B) The continuous collisions of gas particles with the balloon walls create an outward force called pressure
explanation: Gas pressure is created by millions of gas particles moving randomly and constantly bumping into the surfaces around them. These collisions exert a force on the balloon walls, pushing them outward and keeping the balloon inflated. The more collisions per second, the higher the pressure.
